title: Karnak Temple Complex
description: Massive temple complex dedicated to the Theban Triad, spanning over 2000 years of construction.

title: Luxor Temple
description: Ancient temple on the east bank of the Nile, largely built by Amenhotep III and Ramesses II.

title: Luxor Museum
description: Elegant museum displaying a high-quality collection of antiquities like the Luxor Cachette.

title: Mummification Museum
description: Unique museum on the corniche dedicated to the art and science of ancient Egyptian mummification.

title: Avenue of Sphinxes
description: The 2.7 km ancient processional path connecting Karnak and Luxor Temples.

title: Mosque of Abu Haggag
description: Historic mosque integrated into the structure of Luxor Temple, dating back to the Ayyubid period.

title: Valley of the Kings
description: Royal burial ground for pharaohs of the New Kingdom, hidden in the Theban hills.

title: Tomb of Tutankhamun (KV62)
description: Famous tomb of the Boy King found intact by Howard Carter in 1922.

title: Tomb of Seti I (KV17)
description: One of the longest and most beautifully decorated tombs in the Valley of the Kings.

title: Tomb of Ramesses VI (KV9)
description: Spectacular tomb known for its well-preserved astronomical ceilings.

title: Tomb of Ay (WV23)
description: Hidden gem located in the Western Valley (Valley of the Monkeys).

title: Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut
description: Magnificent terraced temple at Deir el-Bahari, built by the female pharaoh Hatshepsut.

title: Colossi of Memnon
description: Two massive stone statues of Amenhotep III that heavily guarded his ruined mortuary temple.

title: Medinet Habu (Temple of Ramesses III)
description: Best preserved mortuary temple on the West Bank, known for its deep reliefs and massive pylons.

title: Ramesseum
description: The mortuary temple of Ramesses II, featuring the fallen colossus that inspired Ozymandias.

title: Valley of the Queens
description: Burial place for royal wives and princes of the New Kingdom.

title: Tomb of Nefertari (QV66)
description: Known as the Sistine Chapel of Ancient Egypt due to its stunningly preserved colorful murals.

title: Deir el-Medina
description: The village of the artisans who built and decorated the royal tombs.

title: Tomb of Sennedjem (TT1)
description: Beautifully preserved artisan's tomb in Deir el-Medina depicting scenes of the afterlife.

title: Tomb of Inherkhau (TT359)
description: Artisan tomb with distinctive artistic style and vivid colors.

title: Temple of Hathor (Deir el-Medina)
description: A small, well-preserved Ptolemaic temple dedicated to Hathor and Maat within the workers' village.

title: Tombs of the Nobles (Sheikh Abd el-Qurna)
description: Final resting places of powerful courtiers and officials, featuring scenes of daily life.

title: Tomb of Rekhmire (TT100)
description: Grand tomb of a Vizier, famous for scenes of foreign tribute bearers.

title: Tomb of Sennefer (TT96)
description: Known as the Tomb of the Vineyards for its remarkable ceiling painted with grapevines.

title: Tomb of Ramose (TT55)
description: Nobleman's tomb showing the transition between traditional and Amarna art styles.

title: Tomb of Nakht (TT52)
description: Small but exquisitely painted tomb of a scribe and astronomer.

title: Tomb of Menna (TT69)
description: Vividly colored tomb of a scribe of the fields, featuring agricultural scenes.

title: Mortuary Temple of Seti I (Qurna)
description: Often overlooked mortuary temple of Seti I on the edge of the cultivation.

title: Temple of Isis at Deir Shelwit
description: Hidden gem: Small Roman-era temple dedicated to Isis, located south of Medinet Habu.

title: Howard Carter House
description: Restored residence of the archaeologist who discovered Tutankhamun's tomb, now a museum.

title: Palace of Malkata
description: Ruins of the vast palace complex of Amenhotep III.

title: Temple of Esna (Khnum)
description: Ptolemaic and Roman temple known for its hypostyle hall with well-preserved astronomical ceiling.

title: Temple of Montu at Tod
description: Hidden gem: Ruins of a temple dedicated to the war god Montu.

title: Temple of Montu at Medamud
description: Archaeological site northeast of Luxor features remains of a temple to Montu.

title: Necropolis of El-Mo'alla
description: Site of the First Intermediate Period tombs of Ankhtifi and Sobekhotep.

title: Tombs of Dra' Abu el-Naga
description: Necropolis area containing tombs mostly from the 17th and 18th Dynasties.

title: Tomb of Roy (TT255)
description: Small New Kingdom tomb in Dra' Abu el-Naga known for its colorful solar scenes.

title: Tomb of Shuroy (TT13)
description: Ramesside tomb in Dra' Abu el-Naga belonging to the head of the brazier-bearers of Amun.

title: Roman Temple at Shanhur
description: Hidden gem: Remains of a Roman temple located north of Luxor near Qus.

title: El-Assasif Tombs
description: Area containing large tombs from the 25th and 26th Dynasties, such as Pabasa.

title: Tomb of Kheruef (TT192)
description: Largest private tomb in the Theban necropolis, dating to the reign of Amenhotep III and IV.

title: Karnak Open Air Museum
description: Located within Karnak, housing reconstructed chapels like the White Chapel.

title: Temple of Thoth Hill
description: Rare archaic temple ruins located on a high peak of the Theban hills.

title: Monastery of St. George (West Bank)
description: Active Coptic monastery near Medinet Habu, built on ancient remains.

title: Tomb of Pashedu (TT3)
description: Hidden gem in Deir el-Medina famous for the scene of the deceased drinking from a palm tree.

title: Meretsegerv (The Peak)
description: The pyramid-shaped mountain overlooking the Valley of the Kings, sacred to the cobra goddess.

title: Temple of Merenptah
description: Mortuary temple of Pharaoh Merenptah, featuring a museum with beautiful reliefs.

title: Gebelein
description: Area south of Luxor with remains of a Temple of Hathor.

title: Chapels of the Divine Adoratrices
description: Mortuary chapels for the God's Wives of Amun located within Medinet Habu.
